fields.label: Bot User OAuth Token
fields.helpText: From Slack App -> OAuth & Permissions -> Bot User OAuth Token

healthcheck_config.description: Validates bot token via Slack auth.test endpoint

metadata.summary: Slack workspace messaging for channels, DMs, and workflow notifications.
metadata.setup_guide:
1. Go to api.slack.com/apps and create a new app (or select existing)
2. Go to 'OAuth & Permissions' in the sidebar
3. Add the bot token scopes your integration needs
4. Click 'Install to Workspace' and authorize
5. Copy the xoxb-... Bot User OAuth Token and paste it here

llm_usage_hint.overview: Slack Web API. Bearer token in $SLACK_BOT_TOKEN. Base URL: https://slack.com/api. Responses always include {"ok": bool, "error": "..." } -- check ok before trusting the payload.
examples: curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $SLACK_BOT_TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8" -d '{"channel":"C01234ABC","text":"Hello"}' https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage

curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $SLACK_BOT_TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8" -d '{"channel":"{channel_id}","blocks":[{"type":"section","text":{"type":"mrkdwn","text":"*Report*"}}]}' https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage
gotchas: Always check the 'ok' field in the response -- Slack returns HTTP 200 with {"ok":false,"error":"..."} for most failures.
Channel IDs (like C01234ABC) work everywhere; channel names with a hash prefix only work in chat.postMessage.
Bot tokens need specific scopes per endpoint: chat:write, channels:read, channels:history, etc. missing_scope errors tell you which.
Use Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8 for POST bodies; form-encoded is legacy.
